name: Mobile Phase 5 — PWA Surface
outcome_goal: A PWA installable from the Cloudflare Tunnel URL renders the Moon-style canvas with BurnRateHeader (pending approvals, burn rate, active-session blast-radius) and five compact panels (Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System). Approval inbox shows plan-envelope previews with a biometric ratify button using the device's platform authenticator. Device enrollment flow from a bootstrap token works end-to-end. Oversight signals (policy_drift_count_24h, autonomy_depth, blast-radius) are surfaced inside existing panels — no new panels.
anti_requirements: Do NOT introduce a native iOS/Android app — PWA served from the same origin as Moon.
Do NOT add panels beyond Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System.
Do NOT introduce charts, sparklines, or tables inside panels — numeric rows only.
Do NOT scroll inside a panel at 844px viewport — drop rows until it fits.
Do NOT widen writes outside surfaces/app/src/mobile/ and surfaces/api/handlers/ + handlers it wires into rest.py.
notes: Lifted verbatim from config/cascade/specs/W_mobile_access_plan_20260420.queue.json wave 5. Depends on Phase 2 (endpoints), Phase 3 (push), Phase 4 (tunnel origin for real Passkey registration).

jobs.label: wave5_pwa_scaffold
jobs.description: Scaffold the mobile PWA under surfaces/app/src/mobile/ with router, manifest, service worker.
jobs.outcome_goal: Navigating a mobile breakpoint to /mobile loads a PWA shell installable from the browser menu; manifest.json declares name 'Praxis Control', black/white theme, single standalone window.
jobs.prompt:
Add the PWA scaffold under Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/.

- main.tsx — React router with routes /mobile, /mobile/approvals, /mobile/flow, /mobile/economy, /mobile/providers, /mobile/quality, /mobile/system, /mobile/enroll, /mobile/settings.
- manifest.json — name 'Praxis Control', short_name 'Praxis', display 'standalone', theme_color '#000000', background_color '#000000', icons pointing to /mobile/icons/*.
- service-worker.ts — registers, caches app shell + static assets only (NO API caching); activates immediately on update (skipWaiting + clients.claim). Reuse the push handler added in Phase 3.
- api/client.ts — fetch wrapper sending cookies (credentials: 'include') and parsing JSON.
- api/webauthn.ts — thin wrapper over navigator.credentials.create/get using the server-issued challenge.
- styles/base.css — black background, white text, hollow circle nodes, subtle type-glow matching the Moon visual language.

Wire the mobile route group into surfaces/app/vite.config.ts so `npm --prefix 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app' run build` outputs both Moon and Mobile bundles.

Verify: npm run build succeeds and emits surfaces/app/dist/mobile/ with manifest.json and sw.js.

jobs.label: wave5_canvas_shell
jobs.description: Moon-style canvas shell: always-visible header (pending approvals + burn rate); five panels collapse-idle expand-on-tap.
jobs.outcome_goal: Opening /mobile shows black canvas with white hollow nodes. Top lines: 'Pending approvals: N' (red dot if >0) and 'Burn rate: $X / $Y, expires HH:MM'. Five panel nodes below: Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System — collapsed by default, expanding on tap.
jobs.prompt:
Build Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/pages/Canvas.tsx and shared header components.

- components/BurnRateHeader.tsx — polls GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot every 5s (or listens on SSE); renders two lines: pending approvals count with badge (zero-state white, >0 red dot), burn rate vs active-grant budget with expiry HH:MM.
- components/PanelNode.tsx — hollow circle node with title; compact-idle shows only label; tap expands to inline content.
- pages/Canvas.tsx — composes BurnRateHeader above vertical stack of five PanelNodes; clicking a node expands inline (no separate page).

Canvas rules (Moon visual language):
- Black background #000, white nodes/text.
- Hollow nodes: 1px white border, no fill, subtle 2px glow.
- One view, one canvas. No tabs, no stacked navigation. Expansion in-place.
- No breadcrumbs, no separate pages beyond router entry points.

Visual test at surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/canvas.visual.test.tsx using vitest + @testing-library/react: renders Canvas, asserts header renders, asserts five PanelNodes present, asserts tap expansion toggles content visibility.

jobs.label: wave5_approval_inbox
jobs.description: Approval Inbox panel: pending list, plan-envelope preview, biometric ratify, revoke.
jobs.outcome_goal: Tapping Pending Approvals badge expands into a list of pending approval_requests showing each plan envelope summary. Tapping Ratify triggers WebAuthn step-up via the device's platform authenticator (fingerprint, face unlock, or device PIN — whatever the OS exposes); on success the grant is issued and the list updates via SSE.
jobs.prompt:
Build Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/pages/ApprovalInbox.tsx and supporting components.

- components/PlanEnvelopePreview.tsx — expandable card: command_type, spec_path, allowed_operations, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend, expires_in, UI risk hint from server (auto/gate/threshold).
- components/RatifyButton.tsx — button that (1) fetches GET /auth/webauthn/assert/begin?purpose=step_up, (2) calls navigator.credentials.get, (3) POSTs /approvals/{id}/ratify with assertion + challenge_id. Shows success -> dismiss item. Shows failure with reason_code.
- components/RevokeButton.tsx — one-tap revoke.
- pages/ApprovalInbox.tsx — connects to SSE /approvals/subscribe, renders pending list newest-first; each item has PlanEnvelopePreview + RatifyButton + RevokeButton.

Tests at surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/approval_inbox.test.tsx using mocked fetch and mocked navigator.credentials.

jobs.label: wave5_five_panels
jobs.description: Five Moon-style metric panels: Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System.
jobs.outcome_goal: Each PanelNode expands into the correct dense one-view summary backed by GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot. No panel has more than ~6 numeric fields; no scrolling on expanded view at 844px.

jobs.label: wave5_enroll_flow
jobs.description: Device enrollment flow from bootstrap token.
jobs.outcome_goal: Nate can (a) generate a single-use 5-min bootstrap token from 'praxis workflow capability issue-bootstrap-token', (b) open /mobile/enroll on his phone, (c) paste the token, (d) register the device's platform authenticator (biometric or PIN) as a Passkey, ending with a mobile session cookie that survives app restart.
jobs.prompt:
Build the device-enrollment flow.

CLI: add 'praxis workflow capability issue-bootstrap-token [--label <label>]' calling an existing POST /auth/bootstrap/issue internal endpoint (invokable only by CLI process) that inserts webauthn_challenges row with purpose='register' and returns the 32-byte URL-encoded token. Print token + QR-code-friendly URL: https://<rp_id>/mobile/enroll?token=<token>.

PWA: pages/Enroll.tsx at /mobile/enroll reads token from URL param or input box, POSTs to /auth/bootstrap/exchange -> receives register challenge, calls navigator.credentials.create, POSTs the attestation to /auth/webauthn/register/complete. On success, server issues the mobile session cookie; redirect to /mobile.

Tests at tests/integration/test_enroll_flow.py: bootstrap round-trip, expired token rejected, already-used token rejected, label stored.

jobs.label: wave5_oversight_signals_in_metrics
jobs.description: Surface policy_drift_count, autonomy_depth, and active-session blast-radius on the mobile canvas (debate pillars 3 + 4).
jobs.outcome_goal: GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot returns quality.policy_drift_count_24h, quality.autonomy_depth, and flow.blast_radius. BurnRateHeader renders a third line showing active-session blast-radius compactly; when gate_triggered_dim non-null the header line turns red. A non-zero policy_drift_count_24h adds a red dot to the pending-approvals badge even when zero pending approvals.
jobs.prompt:
Extend the existing metrics pipeline — no new endpoints, no new pages.

Backend (Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/mobile_metrics.py):
1. Extend snapshot response:
   - quality.policy_drift_count_24h: runtime.capability.policy_drift.recent_drift_count(conn, hours=24).
   - quality.autonomy_depth: runtime.capability.autonomy_chain.current_depth(conn).
   - flow.blast_radius: active session_blast_radius row as {session_id, files_mutated, bytes_written, external_calls, spend_cents, gate_triggered_dim} or null.
2. Do NOT add keys beyond these.

Frontend:
- components/BurnRateHeader.tsx — add a third compact line below the existing two. Format: '{files} files / {calls} calls / ${spend}'. When gate_triggered_dim non-null, render whole line in red prepended with '⚠ '. When no active session, omit the line.
- components/PendingBadge.tsx — red dot also appears when policy_drift_count_24h > 0, independent of pending approval count.
- pages/panels/QualityPanel.tsx — add rows: 'Policy drift 24h' and 'Autonomy depth'.

Tests:
- Backend at tests/integration/test_mobile_metrics_oversight.py: snapshot includes new keys; fixture drift event + active chain at depth 5 + active session with 30 files_mutated; values round-trip.
- Frontend at surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/oversight_header.test.tsx: BurnRateHeader in three states (no active session; under threshold; gate_triggered_dim='files_mutated').
